authoritative_sources.title: 2024 CAPRA National Accreditation Standards Beta Test Version
authoritative_sources.publisher: Commission for Accreditation of Park and Recreation Agencies / National Recreation and Park Association
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CAPRA is the recognized accreditation body for park and recreation agencies, and its standards directly govern recreation records management, program and service administration, public safety posture, and complaint or evidence handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: record intake, program-service documentation control, auditability, and complaint-aware administrative handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane around recreation-program records, rosters, incident documentation, and public-service administration rather than generic office support behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: Rules & Reservation Policies
authoritative_sources.publisher: Recreation.gov / U.S. General Services Administration and federal land-management partners
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recreation.gov is the official reservation platform for federal recreation sites and facilities. Its policies are authoritative public operating guidance for reservation records, payment timing, cancellation windows, no-show handling, and refund behavior in recreation bookings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: reservation intake, payment timing, cancellation-path review, and refund-package boundary control
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps reservation and facility-booking scenarios tied to authoritative booking, cancellation, and refund records rather than ad hoc customer-service promises.

authoritative_sources.title: Federal Trade Commission Announces Final 'Click-to-Cancel' Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator, and this official rule announcement is authoritative for recurring memberships, auto-renewing passes, disclosure, and simple cancellation expectations relevant to recreation memberships.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: membership cancellation handling, recurring-charge boundaries, and consumer-facing communication control
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from inventing difficult cancellation paths or treating recurring recreation memberships as exempt from consumer-protection expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. consumer-finance regulator for billing-error handling and is authoritative for merchant-versus-issuer boundaries relevant to duplicate charges, missing refunds, and payment mismatch in recreation bookings and memberships.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment mismatch triage, missing-refund escalation, and merchant-versus-issuer boundary recognition
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane so payment mismatches become evidence-backed escalations rather than unsupported refund assurances.

authoritative_sources.title: Model Aquatic Health Code
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDC is the primary federal public-health authority for recreational water guidance, and the MAHC is authoritative public guidance for aquatic-facility operations, incident response, contamination handling, and safety-sensitive boundaries in aquatics programs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: aquatics safety recognition, contamination or incident stop conditions, and safety-owner escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Adds recreation-specific safety boundaries so aquatics administration does not drift into safety signoff or incident adjudication.

authoritative_sources.title: Public Playground Safety Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC is the primary U.S. consumer-product safety regulator. Its handbook is authoritative for public playground inspection, maintenance, age-appropriateness, and stop-condition handling that can materially change recreation-program workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: playground and youth-program safety recognition, inspection-aware documentation handling, and unsafe-condition escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from treating unsafe playground or youth-program conditions as ordinary roster maintenance work.

authoritative_sources.title: Fighting Identity Theft with the Red Flags Rule: A How-To Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC Red Flags guidance is an authoritative public control reference for suspicious identity and account-use signals that affect participant-account updates, guardian-contact changes, and refund-reroute requests.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: identity-mismatch triage, suspicious account-change handling, and fraud-sensitive escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane from handling suspicious account or refund changes as routine front-desk record edits.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and recreation-context grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm program type, facility, participant or household context, reservation or registration identifiers, jurisdiction, and whether the request is in scope before acting.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Registration, reservation, and participant-record verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate registrations, rosters, waitlists, waivers, emergency contacts, attendance, payment state, refund requests, and service tickets against the named systems of record.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Administrative maintenance and audit-trail capture
workflow_stages.description: Prepare record update notes, completeness summaries, and owner-tracked status packages only when artifact lineage, permissions, and source-of-truth ownership are clear.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Retention, access, and publication handling
workflow_stages.description: Evaluate whether incident packets, participant notes, guardian records, and official program documents may be disclosed, retained, or routed based on tenant controls and public baseline constraints.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: High-volume seasonal queue administration
workflow_stages.description: Segment camp, league, aquatics, membership, and facility-reservation intake while preserving evidence checks, queue priority, blocker tracking, and owner assignment.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Policy-exception, safety, and fraud escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate unsupported refund requests, pricing exceptions, unsafe-condition signals, aquatics or playground incidents, and suspicious account or refund changes to the correct owner without over-resolving in lane.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

artifacts: household and participant profiles, guardian-authority records, and member or passholder accounts
program catalogs, session schedules, registrations, reservations, rosters, waitlists, facility-booking records, and attendance scans
waivers, releases, emergency contacts, participant acknowledgments, required attachments, and age-band or supervision flags
POS transactions, payment tenders, refunds, credits, transfer requests, promotions, scholarships, no-show markers, and rainout or make-up notes
service tickets, CRM notes, complaint history, incident packets, account holds, and manager or approver notes
access-control scans, swim-band or pass status, equipment checkout records, and document-repository entries
tenant policy extracts for retention, disclosure, refunds, cancellations, waitlist release timing, and safety or fraud routing
systems_of_record: service desk or customer-support case system

allowed: Classify recreation-program work as in scope, blocked, handoff-ready, or unresolved based on documented registration, roster, waiver, attendance, payment, and incident artifacts.

Prepare evidence-backed completeness summaries, record update notes, administrative status notes, governance escalations, and program or membership status summaries without claiming final pricing, refund, safety, or public-records authority.
Request or validate the minimum tenant facts, systems, and source records needed to continue safely.
escalate: Required registration, reservation, attendance, payment, refund, waiver, incident, retention, or participant-account records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires pricing override, refund exception approval, public-records or privacy interpretation, incident signoff, participant reinstatement, or fraud investigation.
Customer harm, unsafe conditions, aquatics contamination, playground hazards, or suspicious account-change signals materially change the safe answer.
Public baseline guidance and tenant or jurisdiction policy materially conflict on cancellation, refunds, disclosure, retention, supervision, or safety handling.
refuse: Backdate attendance, delete incident notations, alter no-show or refund history, or otherwise manipulate official records without documented authority.
Override pricing, refund, waitlist, transfer, access, or safety rules without explicit delegated authority.
Treat recreation-program administration as authority to declare a participant safe, eligible, cleared, or officially reinstated.

domain_failure_modes: the lane updates a roster or account without confirming which booking, registration, or participant record is authoritative
recreation-specific artifacts such as waivers, emergency contacts, incident packets, waitlists, access scans, or rainout credits are collapsed into generic customer-service notes
refund or transfer requests are handled as routine front-desk courtesy instead of approval-bound policy exceptions
participant-safety or unsafe-condition issues are treated as ordinary administrative tasks rather than stop conditions requiring escalation
suspicious guardian or account-change requests are processed without fraud-sensitive review
public booking or cancellation guidance and tenant policy conflict, but the lane still gives a definitive answer
high-volume seasonal intake causes staff to skip evidence validation, owner tracking, or queue segmentation

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Required record or evidence package is incomplete
Retention, privacy, or access rule conflicts with the request
Stakeholder asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant and jurisdiction rules for youth-program guardian access, public-records disclosure, scholarship documentation, aquatics clearance, camp medication records, and private-club membership cancellations can materially narrow the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack uses CAPRA, Recreation.gov, FTC, CFPB, CDC, CPSC, and FTC Red Flags guidance as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve tenant program type, local law, guardian-access rules, waiver templates, refund matrices, and incident-routing owners before applying tenant-specific outcomes.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, systems, and failure handling for recreation administration instead of treating the lane as generic program support.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated package because program-type, jurisdiction, guardian-access, refund, and incident differences are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.

The lane stops at recreation-program administration, evidence-backed maintenance, and escalation packaging; pricing decisions, refund approvals, incident signoff, fraud findings, and safety clearance remain outside the lane.
